steps.title: Compare margin of error
steps.content: The first sample has margin of error $0.8$ minute, and the second sample has margin of error $1.9$ minutes. The first sample therefore has the smaller margin of error.

steps.title: Connect precision to sample size
steps.content: When margins of error are calculated the same way, a larger sample usually gives a smaller margin of error. Since the first sample has the smaller margin of error, it must have contained more air fryers. The answer is A.
